verses.text: A wise woman builds her house, but the foolish plucks it down; he that walks uprightly fears the LORD, and in the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride. Homes rise or fall by wisdom. The reverent walk humbly, while fools talk themselves into trouble.

A wise woman builds her house, but the foolish plucks it down; he that walks uprightly fears the LORD, and in the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride. Homes rise or fall by wisdom. The reverent walk humbly, while fools talk themselves into trouble.

A wise woman builds her house, but the foolish plucks it down; he that walks uprightly fears the LORD, and in the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride. Homes rise or fall by wisdom. The reverent walk humbly, while fools talk themselves into trouble.

Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase comes by their strength; a faithful witness will not lie; 'a scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not,' but knowledge is easy to the discerning. Honesty and effort pay. Wisdom comes readily to the humble, never to the scornful.

Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase comes by their strength; a faithful witness will not lie; 'a scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not,' but knowledge is easy to the discerning. Honesty and effort pay. Wisdom comes readily to the humble, never to the scornful.

Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase comes by their strength; a faithful witness will not lie; 'a scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not,' but knowledge is easy to the discerning. Honesty and effort pay. Wisdom comes readily to the humble, never to the scornful.

Leave the foolish man, for there is no knowledge in his lips; the prudent understands his way, but fools' folly is deceit; 'fools make a mock at sin,' while among the righteous there is favour. Sin is no joke. The wise weigh their path, but fools trifle with what is deadly.

Leave the foolish man, for there is no knowledge in his lips; the prudent understands his way, but fools' folly is deceit; 'fools make a mock at sin,' while among the righteous there is favour. Sin is no joke. The wise weigh their path, but fools trifle with what is deadly.

Leave the foolish man, for there is no knowledge in his lips; the prudent understands his way, but fools' folly is deceit; 'fools make a mock at sin,' while among the righteous there is favour. Sin is no joke. The wise weigh their path, but fools trifle with what is deadly.

The heart knows its own bitterness, and a stranger shares not its joy; the wicked's house is overthrown, the upright's flourishes; 'there is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.' Self-deceit is deadly. What seems right may lead to ruin.

The heart knows its own bitterness, and a stranger shares not its joy; the wicked's house is overthrown, the upright's flourishes; 'there is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.' Self-deceit is deadly. What seems right may lead to ruin.

The heart knows its own bitterness, and a stranger shares not its joy; the wicked's house is overthrown, the upright's flourishes; 'there is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.' Self-deceit is deadly. What seems right may lead to ruin.

Even in laughter the heart may ache, and mirth ends in heaviness; the good man is satisfied from himself; 'the simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.' Discernment guards the soul. The wise test what they are told before they trust it.

Even in laughter the heart may ache, and mirth ends in heaviness; the good man is satisfied from himself; 'the simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.' Discernment guards the soul. The wise test what they are told before they trust it.

Even in laughter the heart may ache, and mirth ends in heaviness; the good man is satisfied from himself; 'the simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.' Discernment guards the soul. The wise test what they are told before they trust it.

A wise man fears and departs from evil, but the fool rages and is confident; the hasty in temper deals foolishly, and the man of wicked devices is hated; the simple inherit folly, the prudent are crowned with knowledge. Caution is wisdom. The reverent shun evil; the rash come to shame.

A wise man fears and departs from evil, but the fool rages and is confident; the hasty in temper deals foolishly, and the man of wicked devices is hated; the simple inherit folly, the prudent are crowned with knowledge. Caution is wisdom. The reverent shun evil; the rash come to shame.

A wise man fears and departs from evil, but the fool rages and is confident; the hasty in temper deals foolishly, and the man of wicked devices is hated; the simple inherit folly, the prudent are crowned with knowledge. Caution is wisdom. The reverent shun evil; the rash come to shame.

The evil bow before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous; the poor is hated even by his neighbour, but the rich has many friends; 'he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.' Mercy outranks money. To show kindness to the needy is blessedness.

The evil bow before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous; the poor is hated even by his neighbour, but the rich has many friends; 'he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.' Mercy outranks money. To show kindness to the needy is blessedness.

The evil bow before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous; the poor is hated even by his neighbour, but the rich has many friends; 'he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.' Mercy outranks money. To show kindness to the needy is blessedness.

Do not they err that devise evil, while mercy and truth attend those who plan good? In all labour there is profit, but mere talk leads to want; the wealth of the wise is their crown, but the folly of fools is folly. Deeds outweigh words. Diligent, well-meant work is rewarded.

Do not they err that devise evil, while mercy and truth attend those who plan good? In all labour there is profit, but mere talk leads to want; the wealth of the wise is their crown, but the folly of fools is folly. Deeds outweigh words. Diligent, well-meant work is rewarded.

Do not they err that devise evil, while mercy and truth attend those who plan good? In all labour there is profit, but mere talk leads to want; the wealth of the wise is their crown, but the folly of fools is folly. Deeds outweigh words. Diligent, well-meant work is rewarded.

A true witness delivers souls, but a deceitful one speaks lies; 'in the fear of the LORD is strong confidence,' a refuge for his children; the fear of the LORD is 'a fountain of life,' to turn from the snares of death. Reverence is security. To fear God is both shelter and the spring of life.

A true witness delivers souls, but a deceitful one speaks lies; 'in the fear of the LORD is strong confidence,' a refuge for his children; the fear of the LORD is 'a fountain of life,' to turn from the snares of death. Reverence is security. To fear God is both shelter and the spring of life.

A true witness delivers souls, but a deceitful one speaks lies; 'in the fear of the LORD is strong confidence,' a refuge for his children; the fear of the LORD is 'a fountain of life,' to turn from the snares of death. Reverence is security. To fear God is both shelter and the spring of life.

A king's honour is in many people; he that is slow to wrath shows great understanding, but the hasty exalts folly; a sound heart is the life of the flesh, and 'he that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker,' but mercy honours God. Patience and mercy are godly. To wrong the poor insults their Maker.

Patience and mercy are godly. To wrong the poor insults their Maker.

Patience and mercy are godly. To wrong the poor insults their Maker.

The wicked is driven away in his wickedness, but the righteous has hope in death; wisdom rests in the discerning heart; 'righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people'; the king favours a wise servant. Righteousness lifts both soul and nation. The godly have hope even in death.

Righteousness lifts both soul and nation. The godly have hope even in death.
